title: Fixes: #28374 de-deuplicated quiet option (docs)
body:
De-duplicates quiet option on `podman push`, `podman pull`, `podman artifact push`, and `podman artifact pull`. Also made quiet option wording more generic so it makes sense with the new dependent docs.

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes, to the wording on the documentation.

```release-note
De-deuplicated `--quiet` option and improved wording.
```

comments.body: Oops, the commit message was too long - I've amended it and pushed
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

@Jessicaward thanks for the PR!  The changes look great, but the commit message seems to be too long still.  What I do in these cases:

1. git commit --amend -s
2.  Count the first 60 chars or so in the message, and add three dots `...` after the next word, keeping the line under 80 char.
3. Hit return twice so that there's a blank line between the shortened description and the rest of the description.
4. git fetch, git rebase, git push --force

HTHs!
I've rebased, which works locally but I can't get it to amend the commit here for some reason! I'll close the PR and open a new one
